patch.value:
Repository setup requirements:
- Description and homepage should match package.json and README.
- Issues should remain enabled for public support.
- Wiki should remain disabled to keep documentation canonical in README and repo files.
- GitHub Projects may be disabled if not needed because pm is the canonical project-management system.
- No paid GitHub features are required or configured.
- Visibility should match the package public-release posture after the secret-history audit is clean.
Align GitHub repository settings for public package release
Use GitHub CLI to verify and update repository metadata, issue/project/wiki settings, visibility, and free-feature-only configuration for pm-changelog.

- gh repo view confirms default branch, visibility, issue/wiki/project settings, URL, and metadata.
- Repository description and homepage are set.
- Repo uses only free GitHub features.
- Visibility is set intentionally after confirming no private data is present in git history.
Updated GitHub repository metadata and free-feature settings with gh; repository is public, issues enabled, projects and wiki disabled, description/homepage/topics set.
GitHub repository settings match package metadata and public release expectations without paid features.
gh repo view reports visibility=PUBLIC, default branch main, issues enabled, projects disabled, wiki disabled, homepage set to README URL, and release-related topics configured.

value.author: codex
value.text: GitHub CLI verification completed after metadata update and public visibility change.

patch.value: GitHub repository metadata and free feature settings are configured and verified with gh.
